description: --output-format (text/json). JSON schema: schema_version, originating_application, entity_count, type_histogram, file_size_bytes, has_properties, has_geometry. For pre-inspection before full import.
title: 6.3: Info command flags

description: Single-quote delimited strings, escaped quotes via '', multi-line string support, raw byte preservation (defer IFC encoding to later step)
title: 1.4: Lexer string literals

description: Parse any STEP/IFC file and produce a stream of Entity structs. No IFC knowledge, no database, just parsing. Deliverable: ifc-to-db parse --dry-run prints entity count, type histogram, and parse duration.
title: Epic 1: STEP Lexer & Entity Parser

description: Parse STEP header for FILE_DESCRIPTION, FILE_NAME, FILE_SCHEMA. Record source filename, file size, schema identifier (e.g. IFC4), originating application, parse timing, entity count. Write to file_metadata table as key-value pairs.
title: 2.4: File metadata extraction

description: Test spatial hierarchy with multi-storey building. Test relationship extraction with known connection patterns. Verify bidirectional traversal: element->container and storey->elements.
title: Relationship testing

description: Use cobra (github.com/spf13/cobra) as CLI framework. Root command: ifc-to-db. Subcommands: import, info, query, schema, version. Every command must support --help.
title: 6.1: CLI framework and command structure

description: Query IFCRELASSOCIATESCLASSIFICATION for classification references, extract classification system name (Uniclass, OmniClass) and item reference
title: Classification extraction

description: Test with extruded solid geometry (most common in IFC). Test placement chain with nested local placements. Test MappedRepresentation (type-level shared geometry). Verify JSON output is valid and re-parseable.
title: 5.6: Geometry testing

description: Identify all entity types starting with IFCREL in raw entities, create config map of rel types to source/target attribute positions (IFCRELAGGREGATES pos 4->5, IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE pos 4->5, IFCRELCONNECTSPATHELEMENTS pos 4->5, IFCRELVOIDSELEMENT pos 4->5, IFCRELFILLSELEMENT pos 4->5, IFCRELASSIGNSTASKS, IFCRELASSIGNSTOGROUP). Extract and insert into relationships table.
title: Generic IFCREL* extraction

description: Report errors with byte offset and line number, on malformed entity: log warning, skip to next semicolon, continue parsing, track statistics: total entities parsed, errors encountered, entities by type
title: 1.9: Entity parser error handling

description: GitHub Actions CI: Linux amd64/arm64, macOS amd64/arm64, Windows amd64. DuckDB static linking per platform. Release archives with checksums.
title: 6.12: Cross-platform builds (CI)

description: Skip whitespace between tokens, handle STEP header section (ISO-10303-21;, HEADER;, ENDSEC;, DATA;, END-ISO-10303-21;), extract header metadata (file description, file name, file schema)
title: 1.7: Whitespace and header handling

description: Query entities for property set IDs from previous step, extract property set name (attr pos 2) and property refs (attr pos 4), resolve each property ref to concrete property entity
title: IFCPROPERTYSET unpacker

description: Add go-duckdb dependency, implement db.Open(path) that creates/opens DuckDB file, run schema DDL on first open (create all tables if not exist), handle CGO build configuration in Makefile
title: 2.1: DuckDB integration setup

description: Query entities for ifc_type=IFCRELDEFINESBYPROPERTIES, extract related element refs (attr pos 4) and property set ref (attr pos 5), build in-memory map: propertySetID -> []elementID
title: IFCRELDEFINESBYPROPERTIES resolver

description: After parsing completes at ~96%, the post-processing phases (properties, relationships, spatial, geometry) run with zero user feedback. Each phase should show a progress indicator: phase name, spinner or elapsed time, and entity/row counts when done. The cache loading step should also indicate progress. Users currently think the tool is stuck after 96%.
title: Add progress indicators to all post-processing phases

close_reason: Implemented and tested with real IFC file
description:
The schema command is a stub that just prints args. Implement it to:
- No args needed — prints the DDL from db/schema.go
- --tables: list just table names
- --table <name>: print DDL for one table
- --columns <name>: list column names and types for a table
- --output-format text|json (FR-015)
- Parse the DDL constants in db/schema.go or use DuckDB information_schema on a temp db
- Designed for agent discoverability (FR-016)
title: Implement schema command

description: Every command --help must include: one-line summary, description paragraph, usage syntax, full flag listing with defaults/types/ranges, 2+ usage examples, exit code docs, related commands. Root --help includes workflow overview.
title: 6.6: Comprehensive help text

description: --output-format (table/csv/json/jsonl), --header/--no-header, --null-value. Accept SQL as positional arg or via --file. Exit codes: 0=success, 1=SQL error, 2=connection error.
title: 6.4: Query command flags

description: Implement \S\ (ISO 8859 shift), \X\ (single hex byte), \X2\...\X0\ (UCS-2 hex), \X4\...\X0\ (UCS-4 hex), \\\\ (literal backslash). Unit tests with known encoded strings from real IFC files.
title: 1.5: IFC string decoding

description: Implement Parser struct with Next() (*Entity, error) method, return io.EOF at end of data section, zero-copy where possible using byte slices referencing memory-mapped source
title: 1.10: Stream interface

close_reason: Implemented: split relConfig into basicRelConfig/deepRelConfig, added deep bool param to ExtractRelationships, added --deep-relationships flag to import command
description: By default emit only 6 spatial/topological relationship types. Add --deep-relationships flag to also emit 4 definition types (IFCRELDEFINESBYPROPERTIES, IFCRELDEFINESBYTYPE, IFCRELASSOCIATESMATERIAL, IFCRELASSOCIATESCLASSIFICATION) when needed.
title: Split relationships into basic and deep tiers

description: Progress lines show trailing characters due to shorter replacement text on \r. Use ANSI erase-to-EOL or pad with spaces.
title: Fix garbled progress output

description: Flags: -o/--output, --memory, --skip-properties, --skip-geometry, --skip-relationships, --skip-quantities, --only, --append, --batch-size, -q/--quiet, -v/--verbose, --log-file, --output-format. Each flag must document purpose, default, valid values/range, and example.
title: 6.2: Import command flags

description: Replace db.Prepare + insert.Exec with DuckDB Appender API in ExtractGeometry. JSON columns passed as string.
title: DuckDB Appender for geometry inserts

description: For simple geometry types (extrusions, boxes), compute axis-aligned bounding box as {min:[x,y,z], max:[x,y,z]}. Skip for complex types (B-rep, CSG) that need a geometry kernel.
title: 5.4: Bounding box computation (optional)

description: Implement Writer struct wrapping DuckDB appender for entities table, batch size configuration (default 10000 entities per flush), automatic flush on batch size threshold and on close
title: 2.2: Batch writer using Appender API

description: Define JSON representation: KindString->string, KindInteger->number, KindFloat->number, KindEnum->{enum:X}, KindRef->{ref:N}, KindList->array, KindTyped->{type:T,value:V}, KindNull->null, KindDerived->{derived:true}. Benchmark json.Marshal vs manual byte-buffer.
title: 2.3: JSON serialization of StepValue

description: E2e test: import real IFC, run expected queries, verify results. Help text tests: every command has examples, every flag has default, --help-json is valid JSON. Output format tests. Exit code tests. Performance regression test.
title: 6.14: CLI testing

description: Given a root entity ID (shape representation), recursively collect all referenced entities. Build JSON tree with id, type, attrs (refs resolved to nested objects). Handle cycles defensively. Set depth limit (50 levels).
title: 5.2: Entity subtree collector

description: Define TokenKind enum and Token struct, implement Lexer struct wrapping []byte with cursor, implement NextToken() (Token, error), handle comment blocks (/* ... */)
title: 1.2: Token definitions and lexer skeleton

description: When importing and the output .duckdb file already exists, the CLI should print a message and ask y/n before deleting. If user passes --force or -f, skip the prompt and replace silently. If stdin is not a terminal (piped), default to abort with a message suggesting --force.
title: Prompt before replacing existing .duckdb file

description: In import.go parse loop: build EntityCache during parse (call cache.Put after writer.Write). Skip 'Loading entity cache' DB phase when cache is pre-populated. Pass pre-built cache to extraction functions.
title: Wire in-memory cache in import.go

description: Integer parsing (optional sign, digit sequence), float parsing (decimal point and/or exponent E/e), edge case: distinguish negative numbers from other uses of -
title: 1.3: Lexer numeric literals

description: Production-ready CLI with comprehensive machine-readable help, structured output, proper error handling, and cross-platform builds. Deliverable: downloadable binaries, --help-json for agent discovery, --output-format json on all commands.
title: Epic 6: CLI Polish & Distribution

description: Query IFCRELASSOCIATESMATERIAL for material assignments, handle IFCMATERIAL, IFCMATERIALLAYERSET, IFCMATERIALLAYERSETUSAGE, extract material names, layer thicknesses, layer ordering
title: Material extraction

description: Query IFCRELAGGREGATES to build tree: IFCPROJECT->IFCSITE->IFCBUILDING->IFCBUILDINGSTOREY->IFCSPACE. Query IFCRELCONTAINEDINSPATIALSTRUCTURE to assign elements to containing storey/space. Build path string for each element (e.g. Project/Site/Building A/Floor 2/Room 201). Compute hierarchy level (0=project, 1=site, etc). Write to spatial_structure table.
title: Spatial hierarchy construction

description: Populate relationships table with all IFCREL* entity connections and build spatial hierarchy tree. Deliverable: SELECT * FROM spatial_structure WHERE parent_type = 'IFCBUILDINGSTOREY' returns all elements on a given floor.
title: Epic 4: Relationship & Spatial Hierarchy

description: Same pattern as property sets for quantity sets. Handle IFCQUANTITYLENGTH, IFCQUANTITYAREA, IFCQUANTITYVOLUME, IFCQUANTITYWEIGHT, IFCQUANTITYCOUNT, IFCQUANTITYTIME. Extract numeric value and unit.
title: IFCELEMENTQUANTITY unpacker

description: ifc-to-db schema prints full DDL. --table prints DDL for specific table. --tables lists table names. --columns lists columns with name/type/description. --output-format json for full schema as JSON. Critical for agent discoverability.
title: 6.5: Schema command

close_reason: Fixed in commit 722ed5b on feat/gui: moved progress.Finish() before post-processing phases so parse progress line is properly terminated before phase output begins.
description: The parse progress bar uses \r (no newline) to update in-place. progress.Finish() was called after all post-processing phases, causing it to overwrite phase output. Phase prints also appended to the live progress line. Fix: move progress.Finish() to immediately after writer.Close(), before any phase output begins.
title: Fix CLI output overwriting itself during progress display

description: Test: parse and import small IFC, verify entity count in DB matches. Test: JSON roundtrip with DuckDB JSON functions. Test: multiple imports (replace vs --append). Benchmark: import 100 MB file measuring total time including DB writes.
title: 2.7: Import testing

description: Batch insert resolved properties into properties table, batch insert quantities into quantities table, include element_type in each row for filtering
title: Write to properties/quantities tables

description: Extract all property sets and quantity sets from raw entity table into flat, queryable tables. Deliverable: SELECT * FROM properties WHERE prop_name = 'FireRating' returns all elements with fire rating data.
title: Epic 3: Property Set Denormalization

description: Serialize each element's geometry representation and placement as JSON blobs for later computation. Deliverable: SELECT element_id, representation_type FROM geometry WHERE representation_type = 'SweptSolid' returns elements with full geometry JSON trees.
title: Epic 5: Geometry Serialization

description: Add Attrs []step.StepValue to CachedEntity, NewEntityCacheEmpty() constructor, Put() method, and GetStepAttrs() accessor. Export ExtractGlobalID in writer.go. This eliminates the JSON round-trip by keeping parsed attrs in memory during import.
title: EntityCache: support in-memory build from parse phase

description: Replace tx.Prepare + stmt.Exec with DuckDB Appender API in ExtractRelationships. Convert sql.NullString to *string for nullable context column.
title: DuckDB Appender for relationships inserts

description: ifc-to-db --help-json outputs full CLI structure as JSON: all commands, all flags with metadata (name, short, type, default, description, valid values, required/optional). Primary interface for autonomous agent discovery.
title: 6.7: Machine-readable help (--help-json)

description: Handle 6 property types: IFCPROPERTYSINGLEVALUE (name, nominal value, unit), IFCPROPERTYENUMERATEDVALUE (name, enum list), IFCPROPERTYLISTVALUE (name, value list), IFCPROPERTYBOUNDEDVALUE (name, upper/lower bounds), IFCPROPERTYTABLEVALUE (name, defining/defined lists), IFCPROPERTYREFERENCEVALUE (name, entity ref). Extract human-readable value as string, preserve IFC type name.
title: Property value extraction

description: Replace tx.Prepare + stmt.Exec with DuckDB Appender API in ExtractSpatialHierarchy. Convert sql.NullString/NullInt64 to pointer types for nullable columns.
title: DuckDB Appender for spatial inserts

description: Unit tests for each token type, unit tests for nested attribute lists/typed values/edge cases, integration test: parse minimal IFC file verify entity count and types, integration test: parse real-world IFC file, fuzz testing on lexer with go test -fuzz
title: 1.11: Testing and validation

description: README with quick start and query cookbook. Common SQL patterns for property/spatial/element queries. Architecture doc. CLI reference auto-generated from --help-json. Agent integration guide.
title: 6.13: Documentation

description: Query IFCRELASSIGNSTOGROUP for group memberships (distribution systems, zones). Store group relationships in relationships table with appropriate context.
title: Group and system extraction

description: Structured error types with context (file position, entity ID, phase). Graceful degradation: log and continue on per-element failures. Summary report at end. JSON error format when --output-format json.
title: 6.9: Error handling hardening

description: Insert one row per element with geometry. Include representation_type as queryable column.
title: 5.5: Write to geometry table

description: Generate man pages from cobra (cobra-doc). Shell completions for bash, zsh, fish, powershell. Flag value completions where applicable.
title: 6.11: Man page and completion generation

description: Write parsed entities into a DuckDB database, populating the entities table and file_metadata. Deliverable: ifc-to-db import file.ifc -o output.duckdb creates a database with populated entities and file_metadata tables.
title: Epic 2: DuckDB Writer & Raw Entity Import

description: --output-format json on import emits summary object: status, input_file, output_file, schema_version, duration_ms, entities_parsed, entities_errored, tables_populated, row_counts, phases, warnings, errors. All errors to stderr, structured output to stdout.
title: 6.8: Structured output for agent consumption

description: Byte-offset-based progress (percentage), entity count and parse rate (entities/sec, MB/s), --quiet flag to suppress, --json-progress for machine-readable output
title: 2.5: Progress reporting

close_reason: Implemented and tested with real IFC file
description:
The query command is a stub that just prints args. Implement it to:
- Accept <database.duckdb> and SQL as positional args, or --file for SQL from file
- Open the DuckDB file read-only and execute the query
- Output formats: table (aligned columns), csv, json, jsonl (FR-015)
- --header/--no-header for table/csv output
- --null-value for custom NULL display
- Proper error handling: exit code 3 for DB errors, exit code 4 for bad SQL
- Stream results row by row for large result sets
title: Implement query command

description: Wire lexer -> parser -> writer in CLI command, memory-map input file using mmap, handle graceful shutdown (SIGINT) - flush pending batch, close database cleanly
title: 2.6: End-to-end pipeline

description: Initialize Go module (ifc-to-db), set up directory structure (cmd/, internal/step/, internal/db/, internal/extract/, internal/ifc/, internal/cli/), configure Makefile with build/test/lint/bench targets, add .golangci-lint.yml
title: 1.1: Project scaffolding

description: Test with IFC files containing known property sets verify exact values, test property inheritance from type objects, test edge cases: empty psets, null values, entity refs. Performance test: property extraction < 5 seconds for 100k elements.
title: Property extraction testing

description: Add ProgressFunc callback to ExtractProperties, ExtractRelationships, ExtractSpatialHierarchy, ExtractGeometry. Wire up in import.go to show counts on stderr (e.g. 'Extracting properties... 2,450 props'). Throttle to 10 updates/sec.
title: Progress callbacks for extraction phases

description: Refactor properties.go, relationships.go, spatial.go, geometry.go to use GetStepAttrs + StepValue accessors instead of GetAttrs + JSON helpers. Also merge duplicate IFCRELDEFINESBYPROPERTIES scan into single pass.
title: Convert extraction functions to use StepValue

description: Replace tx.Prepare + row-by-row stmt.Exec in insertProperties and insertQuantities with DuckDB Appender API (same pattern as writer.go). Significant speedup for large property sets.
title: DuckDB Appender for batch inserts

description: Entity IDs and refs (#digits), type names (uppercase+digits+underscores), enum values (.ELEMENT., .T., .F.), structural tokens ( ) , ; =, special values $ (null) and * (derived)
title: 1.6: Lexer remaining token types

description: For each building element, follow Representation attribute to IFCPRODUCTDEFINITIONSHAPE, then to IFCSHAPEREPRESENTATION entries. Identify representation type: SweptSolid, Brep, Tessellation, MappedRepresentation, Clipping, CSG.
title: 5.1: Representation resolver

description: Query IFCRELDEFINESBYTYPE for type->element mappings, merge type-level properties with element-level (element overrides type), flag source of each property: 'instance' or 'type'
title: IFCRELDEFINESBYTYPE property inheritance

description: Follow ObjectPlacement -> IFCLOCALPLACEMENT -> parent placement chain. Each placement has IFCAXIS2PLACEMENT3D with location, axis, ref direction. Serialize full placement chain as JSON. Optionally compute composed 4x4 transformation matrix.
title: 5.3: Placement chain resolver

description: New file internal/extract/stepval.go with helpers: StepRef, StepRefList, StepString, StepFloat, StepFormatValue, StepFormatValueType. These replace the JSON-based extractRefFromRaw/extractString/etc helpers, working directly on step.StepValue.
title: StepValue accessor helpers

description: Structured logging with slog (Go 1.21+). Levels: error/warn/info/debug. --log-file option. JSON log entries when --output-format json.
title: 6.10: Logging (slog)

description: Parse statement pattern: #ID = TYPENAME(attr, attr, ...);, build StepValue tree for attribute lists (recursive for nested lists and typed values), handle typed values like IFCLENGTHMEASURE(2.5), handle empty lists () and entity refs in attributes
title: 1.8: Entity parser

description: Benchmark: parse 100 MB IFC file measure time and memory, profile with pprof identify allocation hotspots, implement string interning for type names (~800 distinct IFC types), target: 100 MB/s parsing throughput
title: 1.12: Performance baseline

close_reason: Implemented and tested with real IFC file
description:
The info command is a stub that just prints args. Implement it to:
- Accept an IFC file OR a .duckdb file as input
- For IFC files: parse the STEP header to show schema version, originating system, description, and entity count summary (type histogram) without full import
- For DuckDB files: show file_metadata table contents and row counts per table
- Support --output-format text|json (FR-015)
- Use the existing step.NewParser and parser.Metadata() for IFC header parsing
- Use the existing db.Open for DuckDB inspection
title: Implement info command
